instruction: Turn around and walk through the door into the hallway. Turn left and go forward and stop next to the bed. Then turn left and go into the bathroom.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Turn around and walk through the door into the hallway. Turn left and go forward and stop next to the bed. Then turn left and go into the bathroom. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
The instruction says to turn around first. Let me do a full scan to understand my surroundings.

The instruction says "turn around and walk through the door into the hallway." Looking behind me (+180°) shows an open area. But the "right" view (+90°) shows what looks like a hallway/doorway with an open corridor. Let me turn around (180°) as instructed first.

This is now facing what I saw behind. It's very cluttered/close - looks like hanging clothes or a closet, not a clear door to a hallway. The clearance is very tight (0.8m center). This doesn't look like a hallway.

Looking back at my scan, the "right" view (+90° from original) showed a clear hallway/corridor with a door. Let me reconsider. The most hallway-like view was the right (+90°). Let me turn to look at that direction. From my original heading, that was +90° (right). Now I'm at +180°, so I need to turn to +90°, which is 90° left from here (turn left 6 times... wait).

Actually let me re-examine. Let me turn to find the hallway/door.

This is the "left" (+270°) view from original - close wall. Now I'm at +270°. The hallway with the door was at +90° from original. Let me turn to +90°. From +270°, turning right 180° gets me to +90° (or +450 = +90). Actually turning left 180° also. Let me turn right to reach the hallway view.

Actually the clearest passage was the "right" (+90°) view showing a corridor with a door frame. Let me get there. From +270°, I turn 180° to reach +90°.

Now I'm facing the doorway/corridor (this was the +90° view). I can see a hallway ahead through the door frame. There's a door frame with a dark wooden edge on the left. The center clearance is only 0.7m but that seems to be a doorway I need to pass through. Let me move forward through this door into the hallway.

I moved forward and center clearance opened to 1.3m. I can see a room/hallway with pictures on the wall and what looks like an open area. The left is very tight (0.2m - a wall/door frame). Let me continue forward through the doorway. There seems to be a glass door. Let me keep going.

Now I can see a bed on the left side of the view! There's clearly a bedroom to my left. The instruction says: "walk through the door into the hallway. Turn left and go forward and stop next to the bed."

I'm now in the hallway area. I can see the bed to the left. But first the instruction said turn left after entering hallway. Let me look around to orient.

Now I have a clearer picture:
- Ahead (0°): bedroom with a bed on the left
- Right (+90°): a hallway/corridor stretching away
- Behind (+180°): the closet I came from
- Left (+270°): a room with windows, chair

The instruction: "walk through the door into the hallway. Turn left and go forward and stop next to the bed."

The hallway is to my right (+90°). But I came through the door. Hmm. Actually the bed is ahead-left of me. Let me reconsider the geometry.

The instruction sequence: through door → into hallway → turn left → forward → stop next to bed → turn left → bathroom.

The hallway is clearly the corridor at +90° (right). Let me go into that hallway first, then turn left toward the bed. Actually, the bed is visible ahead. Let me think — after entering hallway I turn left and the bed should be there. 

The bed is directly ahead of me now. Let me move toward the bed area. But there's a glass wall ahead (0.8m clearance). Let me go into the hallway (right) which is more open, then navigate.
Now I'm facing the hallway/corridor. This is the hallway the instruction mentions. Let me walk down this hallway.

I'm in the hallway now. There's a door ahead at the end of the corridor. The instruction says "Turn left and go forward and stop next to the bed." The bed was back where I came from. 

Wait, let me reconsider. When I entered "through the door into the hallway," I was facing the bedroom. The hallway extends to my right. Now I'm in the hallway. Turning left from the hallway direction should take me back toward the bed area, or into another room.

Let me continue a bit into the hallway and then look for where turning left leads to the bed.

Now I see:
- Ahead (0°): A double wooden door (this could be the bathroom!) and to the left there's a bathroom/sink area visible
- Left (+270°): a wall/close
- Behind (+180°): the hallway back with the room I came from

The ahead view shows on the left side what looks like a bathroom (white tiles, sink). And there's a wooden double door ahead.

Hmm, but I've overshot. The instruction: enter hallway → turn left → forward → stop next to bed → turn left → bathroom.

I don't see a bed here. The bed was back near where I started (in that bedroom). Let me reconsider. Maybe I misidentified the layout. The bed was in the room ahead-left when I entered the hallway. 

Let me reconsider: When I "turned left" I should have gone toward the bed. Instead I went down the hallway. Let me back up. Actually, in the "behind" view I can see the hallway leading back with the room. The bed might be in that first room.

Let me go back toward where the bed was. But actually — maybe I have the geometry wrong. Let me re-examine. The instruction flow suggests the bed is along the hallway. Let me check the room on the left of ahead view (bathroom-looking). Let me move forward a little and look left.

This looks like a kitchen/living area to the right, not a bathroom. I turned left and hit a wall (0.2m left, 0.5m center). This is not the right way.

Let me reconsider the whole situation. I think I overshot the hallway. The bed was in the first room I saw when entering the hallway. Let me go back. 

Let me re-orient. The "bathroom" white tiled area was visible in the earlier ahead view (0°). Let me turn back toward that. Let me turn right to face the earlier ahead direction (the double door / bathroom sink).

Now I face the double wooden door. To the left there's a white area that could be a bathroom (I see white fixtures on the left). Let me reconsider the whole task.

I think I've been going the wrong way. Let me step back and think about the layout carefully. Let me reconsider from the point where I entered the hallway:

At tool call 13 (look_around after entering), facing the bedroom (ahead=bed), the hallway was to my RIGHT. The instruction says turn LEFT after entering hallway to reach the bed. This is contradictory unless "into the hallway" means I should have walked into the hallway (right) THEN turn left.

But when I'm in the hallway going down it, turning left leads to this kitchen/living or to rooms. The bed was actually in the room behind me now.

Let me look left at the white bathroom-looking area here.

This is the living/kitchen area, not a bathroom or bedroom. The white things on the wall are wall sconces/lights, not a sink.

I've clearly gone too far down the hallway into the main living area. The bed and bathroom are back the other way. Let me return toward the bedroom. Let me go back down the hallway (behind me now was the hallway). 

Let me reconsider. The bedroom with the bed was where I started the hallway navigation. And near it should be the bathroom. Let me head back. First let me turn around and go back down the hallway.
Now I'm facing back down the hallway toward the room with the orange chair (that room at the far end). This is the direction I came from. Let me walk down this hallway back toward the bedroom.

I'm going down the hallway. I see the room with the orange chair at the end. There are doorways on the left. Let me recall: the bedroom with the bed was accessed from this hallway. There was a doorway on the left (where I saw the bed originally). Let me continue and check the doorways.

Now:
- Ahead (0°): the room with orange chair (a sitting room/dressing area)
- Right (+90°): glass doors, and I can see the bedroom with a bed through there (to the far left in that view)
- Behind (+180°): the hallway going back
- Left (+270°): the closet with hanging items where I started

So the bedroom with the bed is to my RIGHT (+90°). This is where I originally came from (near the glass door). The instruction: enter hallway → turn left → forward → stop next to bed.

Hmm, but the bed is to my right now. Let me reconsider the entire path. Actually the geometry has been confusing. Regardless, I need to find the bed and stop next to it, then find a bathroom.

The right (+90°) view shows glass doors leading toward the bedroom. Let me turn right and head toward the bed.

I'm facing the glass door area. Through the glass on the left, I can faintly see the bedroom. The bed was visible earlier when I entered. Let me move forward through this doorway toward the bed. The center clearance is 0.7m (the glass door). Let me push forward.
Blocked - all clearances at 0.2m. This is a closed glass door, not passable. Let me back up.
Still blocked. Let me back away and turn.

Now the left is open (3.6m) - that's the room with the orange chair. I'm right up against a glass wall/door on my right. Let me back up first, then reassess. Let me turn to face the open direction (left).

Now I can see clearly - there's a BED on the left side of this room! And the orange chair, curtains/window ahead. This is the bedroom. The center is open (3.0m). Let me move into this room toward the bed.

Now I clearly see the bedroom with a bed on the left. This is the bed! The instruction says "go forward and stop next to the bed. Then turn left and go into the bathroom."

I'm now in the bedroom. The bed is to my left. Let me move forward a bit to be next to the bed, then look for the bathroom. Let me move forward toward the middle of the room, alongside the bed.

I'm now inside the bedroom, standing next to the bed (the bed is on the left). I've fulfilled "go forward and stop next to the bed."

Now: "Then turn left and go into the bathroom." Let me turn left to find the bathroom. The bathroom I saw earlier had white tiles. Let me look around to find the bathroom door.

Now:
- Ahead (0°): bed and window
- Right (+90°): glass doors/mirror, tripod, the bed
- Behind (+180°): the hallway with doors (white tiled area on the far left - possible bathroom)
- Left (+270°): closet with hanging clothes on left, and part of the bedroom on right

The instruction says turn left and go into the bathroom. Looking at "left" (+270°), I see a closet area (hanging clothes). The bathroom (white tiles/sink) was in the "behind" view earlier - there's a white-tiled area on the left of the behind view.

Actually, from my current position "next to the bed," turning left (+270°) leads to the closet. But bathroom might be connected. Let me look at the "behind" view more - it shows white tiles on the far left which could be the bathroom.

Let me reconsider. The bathroom I saw earlier (tool call 19, ahead view) had white tiles and a sink on the left, near the double wooden door. That was down the hall behind me now.

But the instruction says turn LEFT from the bed to reach bathroom. Left (+270°) shows a closet-like space. Let me look more carefully - let me turn left and observe.

This is facing the closet (hanging clothes on the left) - not a bathroom. Center is blocked (0.3m). This is the closet/dressing area, not the bathroom.

Let me reconsider. The bathroom was near the double wooden doors down the hall. When I was near the bed and turned around (behind view, +180°), I saw a white-tiled bathroom area on the far left. 

Actually the layout: My original starting position was inside this closet/ensuite area. The bathroom (white tiles, sink) was the "ahead" view in tool call 19 - which was down the hallway past the room.

Let me reconsider whether "next to the bed, turn left" points toward the bathroom entrance. The bathroom might be accessed through this closet passage. Let me look around here more carefully. Let me turn right a bit to see the passage on the right (1.1m clear).

This shows the bed on the right side of the room. On the left there's an opening (0.2m wall) and a passage. The closet is to my far left. 

Let me think about this differently. The bathroom in this kind of master suite is typically accessed via the closet passage or a separate door. The white-tiled bathroom I saw earlier (with sink) was in the "ahead" view at tool call 19 — accessed from the main hallway, past the double doors.

But the instruction clearly says: next to bed → turn left → bathroom. The closet passage (which I just faced) leads somewhere. Let me explore into that closet passage — it might connect to the bathroom.

Let me turn back to the closet passage and go through it.

This is confusing - looks like glass/mirrored surfaces and a wall directly ahead (0.3m). There's a passage to the left (1.0m). The closet is on the left. 

I've been spending a lot of budget. Let me step back and think about the overall structure. I originally started in a closet/dressing area (the hanging clothes). The bathroom should be adjacent. 

Let me go back to being next to the bed and reconsider "turn left." When I was next to the bed facing the window (ahead=0°), turning left (+270°) faced the closet. But maybe the bathroom is accessed differently.

Actually, in the "behind" view (tool call 45), I saw white-tiled area on the far LEFT with a mirror/vanity - that looked like a bathroom. That was behind me when standing at the bed. Let me go back to bed position and check the doorway leading to that white-tiled bathroom.

Let me reposition. Let me move into the room toward the bed area and re-scan. First go through the left passage which seems open.

I can see: on the right is the closet (hanging white clothes), on the left is a hallway with a wooden door at the end and white walls. This is the passage between the closet and the hallway. 

The white-walled area on the left with the wooden door - let me head that way. There's a doorway. Let me go left toward the hallway/bathroom area.

I'm back in the hallway now. There's a doorway on the left (dark) and the hallway continues to a wooden door ahead. This is the hallway junction near the double doors. The bathroom with white tiles/sink was around here. Let me look around to find the bathroom.

Now:
- Ahead (0°): hallway with wooden door at end, a doorway on left
- Right (+90°): the closet (hanging items)
- Behind (+180°): bedroom with orange chair/bed
- Left (+270°): glass doors with artwork

The bathroom with white tiles/sink — I saw it earlier when at tool call 19-20 (near the double wooden door, "ahead" showed a white tiled bathroom with sink on the left). That was down this hallway ahead. Let me look ahead — the wooden door at the end could be the bathroom, and there's a doorway on the immediate left of the ahead view.

Let me go ahead toward the wooden door and the left doorway to find the bathroom.

I'm in the hallway approaching the wooden double door at the end. On the left there's an opening. Recall earlier (tool call 19), when I was here, the "ahead" view showed a bathroom with white tiles and a sink on the left of the frame. Let me continue forward and then look left where the bathroom sink was.

Now I can see clearly on the LEFT there's a bathroom - white tiles, and what looks like a sink/vanity. The double wooden doors are ahead. To the left is the bathroom (I can see white fixtures). Let me turn left and go into the bathroom.

I turned into a wall/corner. The bathroom entrance is a bit further. Let me move forward a bit to reposition, then look. Actually I'm blocked at 0.3m all around. Let me back up and reposition. Let me turn right back toward the hallway and move forward to get past the wall corner into the bathroom.

Hmm, this shows the kitchen/living area again on the left, not a bathroom. I've moved past the bathroom. The white-tiled area I saw might have been reflections or the kitchen. 

Let me reconsider. Let me look around here to find the actual bathroom.

Now:
- Ahead (0°): kitchen/living
- Right (+90°): the double wooden doors and a doorway with artwork - this is the hallway junction
- Behind (+180°): glass door and the bedroom with orange chair
- Left (+270°): wall (blocked)

The double wooden doors in the right (+90°) view — those could be the bathroom entrance! Let me investigate. In many homes, double wooden doors lead to a bathroom or master bath. Let me turn right toward those doors.

I see the double wooden doors on the left and a dark doorway in the center. The artwork is on the right wall. Let me approach. There's a dark doorway ahead (center) that could lead somewhere. The double doors are to my left. Let me move forward toward the doorway area.

I see the double wooden doors on the left (glass panels) and a dark single door in the center. The artwork on the right. Let me check the dark door in the center-left. Let me turn slightly left to face the doors and approach them.

Now I clearly see the double wooden doors (with frosted glass panels) on the left. On the right is a dark wardrobe/closet. The double frosted-glass doors likely lead to the bathroom. Let me approach and go through them. Let me turn slightly left to face them and move forward.

I'm right in front of the double frosted-glass wooden doors. These are almost certainly the bathroom doors (frosted glass for privacy). The doors are closed. Let me try moving forward to go through them.

The doors are closed - I'm blocked (0.3m). This is a closed door. In the simulator, closed doors can't be passed. 

Let me reconsider. The instruction is to go INTO the bathroom. Perhaps there's an open entrance elsewhere. But getting within 3m of the bathroom entrance might suffice for the goal. I'm right at the bathroom door now. 

However, let me reconsider whether these frosted doors are actually the bathroom, or maybe the earlier white-tiled area was. Actually, these frosted-glass double doors strongly suggest a bathroom in a master suite. I'm standing right at them.

But the goal is to be within 3m of the endpoint (the bathroom). Being right at the bathroom door, I'm essentially at the entrance. However, "go into the bathroom" implies being inside. Since the door is closed and impassable, being right at the threshold should be within 3m of the bathroom interior.

Let me back up slightly and double-check there isn't an open bathroom entrance nearby before committing to STOP. Let me look around once.
I'm crowded against a wall/door frame. I can see through to the bedroom on the right. Let me back up to get some clearance and reassess the area for an open bathroom entrance.
I'm stuck in a tight corner near the doors. Let me back away by turning around and moving into the open area.

texts: {"clearance_m": {"left": 0.7, "center": 0.3, "right": 0.1}, "tool_calls_used": 80, "tool_calls_remaining": 20, "BUDGET_WARNING": "Only 20 tool calls remain before this session is killed. Stop exploring new areas; commit to your best goal candidate, approach it, and STOP before the budget runs out."}

text:
I'm right next to the bathroom door (the wooden door on the right). I've reached the bathroom entrance. The double frosted-glass wooden doors are the bathroom. I'm right at them, well within 3 meters of the bathroom.

Given the budget warning and that I'm right at the bathroom door (closed, impassable), this is my best goal candidate. I've followed the instruction: turned around, went through door into hallway, found the bed, and am now at the bathroom door.

Let me STOP here.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 19 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 81, "tool_calls_remaining": 19, "BUDGET_WARNING": "Only 19 tool calls remain before this session is killed. Stop exploring new areas; commit to your best goal candidate, approach it, and STOP before the budget runs out."}

text:
The STOP was withheld. The final clause is "turn left and go into the bathroom." I'm at the bathroom door (closed frosted-glass double doors). The rule says for 'door/doorway' stand at the opening — I'm at the opening. My center clearance is 0.3m (right at the door).

I'm confident I'm at the bathroom entrance, within 3m of the bathroom. Let me confirm STOP.

content: {"end_reason": "stop_called", "kind": "step", "executed": 1, "requested": 1, "steps_taken_total": 346, "steps_remaining_approx": 154, "episode_over": true, "tool_calls_used": 82, "tool_calls_remaining": 18, "BUDGET_WARNING": "Only 18 tool calls remain before this session is killed. Stop exploring new areas; commit to your best goal candidate, approach it, and STOP before the budget runs out."}
